concepts.explanation: Using a programming language to communicate instructions to a computer, which are then converted into binary machine code.
Writing code in human-readable, English-like syntax rather than complex machine-level binary instructions.
Translating and executing code line-by-line at runtime, making debugging easier but execution slightly slower compared to compiled languages.
Assigning data to variables without explicitly declaring their data type, allowing the type to change dynamically during runtime.
Structuring code using classes as templates or blueprints to create specific objects, improving modularity and real-world mapping.
Using the built-in print function within an IDE to output text strings to the console.
